summary: Production-ready office attendance tracker React app with swipe-in/out logging, automatic working hour calculations (42h 30m weekly target, 7AM-7PM window), core hours enforcement (9:30AM-4PM), 30-minute lunch deduction on regular working days, breakfast bonus (+30min), leave management (full-day, half-day first/second), weekend detection and compensation, smart swipe-out predictions, daily/weekly/monthly dashboard, leave history screen, analytics with charts, CSV/Excel export, push notification reminders, email auth with cloud sync/backup/restore, and premium dark/light mode mobile-first UI.
architectural_notes: React PWA (mobile-first) with offline-first architecture using IndexedDB/localStorage for local persistence and cloud sync
Working hours only counted between 7:00 AM–7:00 PM; swipe-in before 7AM clips to 7AM, swipe-out after 7PM clips to 7PM
Breakfast Yes = +30 min added to daily hours; core hours 9:30AM–4:00PM must be satisfied or warning shown

Leave deductions: Full-day = -8h30m target, Half-day first half = -4h30m target (swipe-in auto-set to 1PM if 12:30–1PM), Half-day second half = -4h00m target (swipe-out auto-set to 12:30PM if 12:30–1PM)
Weekend compensation: weekend hours (7AM–7PM) count toward weekly total and reduce deficit
Smart swipe-out prediction calculates required swipe-out time based on remaining weekly hours, already-logged hours, and today's breakfast selection
Email authentication with cloud sync, backup, and restore functionality
Analytics module with weekly/monthly charts, avg daily/weekly hours, highest/lowest day stats
Export feature: CSV and Excel with date, swipe-in, swipe-out, hours, breakfast, leave type columns
known_issues: First-half leave calculation incorrectly computes net hours; 13:00→16:00 should yield 3h 00m but does not.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- SwipeTrack Pro app icon — dark charcoal circle background with a stylised amber/gold clock-and-checkmark motif, clean flat design (swipetrack-icon.dim_512x512.png)
- SwipeTrack Pro app icon — same design, maskable safe-zone version with extra padding (swipetrack-icon-maskable.dim_512x512.png)
- SwipeTrack Pro app icon — small size variant, same design (swipetrack-icon-192.dim_192x192.png)
- SwipeTrack Pro app icon — medium size variant, same design (swipetrack-icon-144.dim_144x144.png)
- SwipeTrack Pro app icon — small size variants for manifest (swipetrack-icon-96.dim_96x96.png)
- SwipeTrack Pro app icon — smallest manifest size (swipetrack-icon-48.dim_48x48.png)
- SwipeTrack Pro Apple touch icon — rounded square, dark charcoal with amber motif (swipetrack-apple-touch-icon.dim_180x180.png)

existing_features.title: Implement all working-hour calculation logic in the frontend as a shared utility module. Rules: (1) Effective working window is 7:00 AM–7:00 PM; clamp swipe-in to 07:00 if earlier, clamp swipe-out to 19:00 if later. (2) Raw hours = swipeOut − swipeIn (after clamping). (3) If breakfastAtOffice is true, add 30 minutes. (4) Full-day leave → daily hours = 0, weekly target reduction = 8h 30m. (5) Half-day first half: if swipeIn is between 12:30–13:00 auto-set to 13:00, target reduction = 4h 30m, no lunch deduction. (6) Half-day second half: if swipeOut is between 12:30–13:00 auto-set to 12:30, target reduction = 4h 00m, no lunch deduction. (7) Default weekly target = 42h 30m; adjusted target = 42h 30m − sum of leave reductions for the week. (8) Core hours = 9:30 AM–4:00 PM; flag violation if swipeIn > 09:30 or swipeOut < 16:00.

Build a Daily Entry screen (primary screen on mobile) with: (1) Date picker defaulting to today. (2) Swipe-in time input with 'Swipe In Now' quick button filling current time. (3) Swipe-out time input with 'Swipe Out Now' quick button filling current time. (4) Breakfast toggle (Yes/No). (5) Leave type dropdown: No Leave, Half-Day Leave – First Half, Half-Day Leave – Second Half, Full-Day Leave. (6) Save button that persists the record to the backend. (7) Inline display of calculated daily hours, any core hours warning, and real-time smart swipe-out prediction.

Implement the Smart Swipe-Out Prediction feature. After swipe-in is entered (and breakfast selection is known), compute and display: (a) remaining hours needed this week = adjusted weekly target − hours already completed this week (Mon–today, excluding today's incomplete entry); (b) required swipe-out time today = swipeIn + remaining hours − breakfast bonus if applicable; (c) Display as 'You need Xh XXm more this week. Swipe out at HH:MM to complete your weekly target.' Update prediction live when swipe-in or breakfast changes.

Build a Dashboard screen with three tabs: Today, Week, and Month. Today tab: swipe-in, swipe-out, daily hours, core hours status. Week tab: weekly target, completed hours, remaining hours, deficit (if any), a visual progress bar, weekly status indicator (On Track in green / Behind Schedule in red), and weekend compensation message ('You have XXh XXm deficit. Work on weekend.') when applicable. Month tab: total monthly hours, count of working days, average daily hours.

Build a Leaves screen that lists: all full-day leaves (date, type), all half-day leaves (date, type), and a complete chronological leave history. Allow tapping a leave entry to view full details. Show a summary count of total full-day and half-day leaves taken.

Build an Analytics screen showing: (1) A bar or line chart of daily hours for the current week. (2) A bar or line chart of weekly total hours for the current month. (3) Stats panel: average daily hours, average weekly hours, highest single day, lowest single day (excluding leaves and weekends).

Implement CSV and Excel export. When the user triggers export from a dedicated Export screen or menu, generate a downloadable file containing columns: Date, Swipe-In, Swipe-Out, Hours, Breakfast (Yes/No), Leave Type. Allow user to select export range: current week, current month, or all records. Provide both CSV and Excel (XLSX) format options.

Implement in-app notification reminders using browser Notification API (with permission request on first use): a 10:00 AM reminder 'Don't forget to swipe in' and a 6:30 PM reminder 'Don't forget to swipe out'. Schedule reminders daily using setTimeout/setInterval calibrated to the next occurrence. Notifications should only fire on weekdays.

Implement Internet Identity authentication as the login mechanism. Users must authenticate via Internet Identity before accessing any data. All backend calls must use the authenticated principal as the user identifier so records are scoped per user.

Implement offline-first data handling using localStorage as an offline cache. When the backend is unreachable, save records to localStorage. On reconnection/app load, sync any pending local records to the backend automatically. Display a subtle sync status indicator (e.g., 'Syncing…' / 'Synced') in the UI.

Implement light mode and dark mode theming with a toggle accessible from a settings or profile menu. The selected theme must be saved to localStorage and applied on next app load automatically. All screens and components must respect the active theme.

Apply a premium, mobile-first visual design throughout the app. Use a sophisticated dark charcoal and warm gold/amber accent palette for dark mode, and clean off-white with deep slate and amber accents for light mode. Use rounded cards with soft shadows, smooth page transition animations, modern sans-serif typography with clear hierarchy, and a bottom tab navigation bar for mobile (Today, Dashboard, Leaves, Analytics, Export tabs). Ensure all interactive elements have smooth hover/press states and the layout is optimized for one-handed phone use.
Generate a custom app icon and a hero/splash visual for the SwipeTrack Pro app.

Update the working-hour calculation logic in `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts` to automatically deduct 30 minutes for lunch on regular working days (leaveType = #noLeave / full-day presence). The updated net hours formula must be: Net Hours = (swipeOut − swipeIn, after clamping) − 30 min lunch + 30 min breakfast bonus (if breakfastAtOffice is true). Half-day leave entries (first half and second half) must NOT have the lunch deduction applied, as per the existing rules. Full-day leave entries continue to return 0 daily hours with no deduction.

In the Daily Entry screen (frontend/src/pages/DailyEntry.tsx), add a validation rule for first-half leave: if leaveType is halfDayFirstHalf and the entered swipeIn time is after 13:00, display an inline warning message 'Apply leave for full day' near the swipe-in field. The record must still be saveable — this is a non-blocking advisory warning only.

In the Daily Entry screen (frontend/src/pages/DailyEntry.tsx), add a blocking validation rule for second-half leave: if leaveType is halfDaySecondHalf and the entered swipeOut time is before 12:30, display an inline error message 'You cannot leave before 12:30' near the swipe-out field and disable the Save button until swipeOut is 12:30 or later.

Update the working-hour calculation logic in frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts to handle second-half leave days where the user also works after 13:00. For a halfDaySecondHalf entry with swipeOut after 13:00, calculate the extra hours as (clampedSwipeOut − 13:00) and add them to the day's worked hours and to the weekly hours total, regardless of whether there is any weekly deficit. The existing 4h 00m target reduction for second-half leave still applies.

Add a daily hours visual indicator throughout the app (Daily Entry inline display, Dashboard Today tab, and Dashboard Week day rows). If the day's calculated hours are ≥ 8h 30m, display the hours value in green and append the surplus in green (e.g., '9h 00m +0h 30m'). If the day's calculated hours are < 8h 30m (and it is a regular working day, not a full-day leave), display the hours value in red and append the shortfall in red (e.g., '7h 50m −0h 40m'). Full-day leave entries should not show a red indicator.

Update the Dashboard screen (frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx) Week tab to display a day-by-day breakdown for the full current week (Monday through Sunday). Each day row must show: the day name and date, the hours worked for that day, and the green/red visual indicator (as defined in REQ-19). Days with no record yet should show '—' or '0h 00m' in a neutral style. The existing weekly summary stats (target, completed, remaining, progress bar, on-track indicator, deficit message) must remain above or below the day-by-day list.
Deploy the SwipeTrack Pro application (backend and frontend) to the Internet Computer mainnet/production network, making it publicly accessible.

Implement an Apple-style full-screen calendar overlay for the date picker in the Daily Entry screen (frontend/src/pages/DailyEntry.tsx). When the user taps the date field, open a full-screen modal overlay with a 40% transparent/frosted-glass background (backdrop with rgba or bg-opacity-40 blur effect). The modal must contain a large monthly calendar grid with: clean minimal Apple-inspired typography, muted weekday header labels (S M T W T F S), a subtle 'today' indicator, and an elegant filled-circle highlight on the selected day. Include smooth open/close CSS transition animations (fade + slide-up). Dismiss the overlay on swipe-down gesture or tap outside the calendar card. The calendar card itself should have a solid (non-transparent) surface so day labels and numbers remain fully readable against the transparent backdrop.

Update `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts` to refine the First-Half Leave calculation: the effective working window must start at 13:00 (ignore actual swipe-in time entirely — treat it as 13:00 regardless of what was entered). Net hours = clampedSwipeOut (capped at 19:00) − 13:00. No breakfast bonus must be applied for first-half leave entries, even if breakfastAtOffice is true. No lunch deduction. Target reduction remains 4h 30m.

Update `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts` to refine the Second-Half Leave calculation: the effective working window ends at 12:30 (clamp swipe-out to 12:30 for the primary block). Net hours for the primary block = 12:30 − clampedSwipeIn (min 07:00). If swipe-out is after 13:00, add extra hours = clampedSwipeOut − 13:00 on top of the primary block (existing rule preserved). Breakfast bonus (+30 min if breakfastAtOffice is true) applies as normal. No lunch deduction. Target reduction remains 4h 00m.

Fix the second-half leave calculation in `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts` so that the primary block net hours are computed as `12:30 − clampedSwipeIn (min 07:00)` without any further deductions or adjustments that reduce the result. For the example swipe-in 08:00 → swipe-out 12:30 with leaveType halfDaySecondHalf: clampedSwipeIn = 08:00, primary block = 12:30 − 08:00 = 4h 30m. No lunch deduction applies. Breakfast bonus (+30 min if breakfastAtOffice is true) still applies on top of the primary block. If swipe-out is after 13:00, extra hours = clampedSwipeOut − 13:00 are added on top of the primary block as before. Target reduction remains 4h 00m. The result of 4h 30m for the 08:00→12:30 example must be treated as meeting the half-day expectation and displayed in green.

Fix the first-half leave calculation in `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts` so that net hours are computed as clampedSwipeOut (capped at 19:00) − 13:00, with the effective window always starting at 13:00 regardless of the actual swipe-in time entered. For example, swipe-in 13:00 → swipe-out 16:00 must yield exactly 3h 00m. No breakfast bonus must be applied for halfDayFirstHalf entries even if breakfastAtOffice is true. No lunch deduction applies. Target reduction remains 4h 30m.

Update `frontend/index.html` to include a full PWA manifest link (`<link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'>`), theme-color meta tag matching the app's amber/charcoal palette, Apple mobile web app meta tags (`apple-mobile-web-app-capable`, `apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style`, `apple-mobile-web-app-title`), and Apple touch icon links for common iOS sizes (180×180, 152×152, 120×120).

Create `frontend/public/manifest.json` as a fully PWA-compliant Web App Manifest with: `name: 'SwipeTrack Pro'`, `short_name: 'SwipeTrack'`, `description: 'Office attendance and working-hours tracker'`, `start_url: '/'`, `display: 'standalone'`, `orientation: 'portrait'`, `theme_color` and `background_color` matching the app's dark charcoal/amber palette, and an `icons` array covering sizes 48, 72, 96, 128, 144, 152, 192, 384, and 512 pixels — including at least one entry with `purpose: 'maskable'` for the 192×192 and 512×512 sizes pointing to generated icon assets in `frontend/public/assets/generated/`.

Add a service worker registration script to `frontend/index.html` (or `frontend/src/main.tsx` via a registration helper) that registers `/sw.js` if the browser supports service workers. Create a minimal `frontend/public/sw.js` that caches the app shell (index.html, main JS/CSS bundles) on install and serves them from cache when offline, ensuring the app meets the PWA installability criteria required by the Play Store via TWA.

Fix the green/red daily hours color indicator threshold logic throughout the app (`frontend/src/pages/DailyEntry.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` Today tab, and Dashboard Week day rows). For `halfDayFirstHalf` leave entries, use 4h 30m (270 minutes) as the green/red threshold instead of the full-day 8h 30m. For `halfDaySecondHalf` leave entries, use 4h 00m (240 minutes) as the threshold. For `noLeave` (regular working days), keep the existing 8h 30m threshold. Full-day leave entries must never show a red indicator regardless of hours. The surplus/shortfall appended value (e.g., '+0h 30m' or '−0h 40m') must also be computed relative to the correct per-leave-type threshold.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a Motoko backend actor that stores attendance records with the following fields per entry: date (Text), swipeIn (Text), swipeOut (Text), breakfastAtOffice (Bool), leaveType (variant: #noLeave, #halfDayFirstHalf, #halfDaySecondHalf, #fullDayLeave), and a stable HashMap keyed by userId+date. Expose CRUD functions: saveRecord, getRecord, getRecordsByDateRange, deleteRecord, getAllRecords.

Implement Apple-style full-screen calendar overlay for the date picker: full-screen modal with 40% transparent background (frosted/glass aesthetic), smooth open/close animation, large month grid, elegant day selection highlight, swipe-down or tap-outside dismiss. Apply to Daily Entry screen date picker.

Deploy the updated SwipeTrack Pro application (frontend with the new Apple-style calendar overlay) to the Internet Computer mainnet/production network, replacing the current live deployment and making the update publicly accessible.

Update hoursCalculation.ts: First-half leave = working window 13:00→clampedSwipeOut, no breakfast bonus, no lunch deduction, 4h30m target reduction. Second-half leave = working window clampedSwipeIn→12:30 (plus extra after 13:00), breakfast bonus applies, no lunch deduction, 4h00m target reduction.

Fix color indicator logic for half-day leave days: use the reduced half-day target as the green/red threshold. First-Half Leave threshold = 4h 30m (green if hours ≥ 4h30m, red if less). Second-Half Leave threshold = 4h 00m (green if hours ≥ 4h00m, red if less). Do not compare against the full 8h 30m daily target for these leave types.

Fix the green/red color indicator logic for half-day leave days throughout the app (Daily Entry inline display, Dashboard Today tab, and Dashboard Week day rows). For First-Half Leave days, use 4h 30m as the threshold: display hours in green if calculated hours ≥ 4h 30m, and in red if less (e.g., 3h 00m must show in red). For Second-Half Leave days, use 4h 00m as the threshold: display hours in green if calculated hours ≥ 4h 00m, and in red if less. Do not compare half-day leave entries against the full-day target of 8h 30m. Full-day leave entries continue to show no red indicator.

Fix first-half leave calculation in hoursCalculation.ts: for halfDayFirstHalf entries, net hours = clampedSwipeOut − 13:00 (e.g., 13:00→16:00 = 3h 00m). No breakfast bonus, no lunch deduction. Effective window always starts at 13:00 regardless of actual swipe-in.

Update PWA manifest and app icons to make SwipeTrack Pro Play Store-ready: full manifest.json with proper name, theme, standalone display mode, orientation, start URL; full icon set (48px–512px including 192 and 512), maskable icons, Apple Touch Icons, splash screen/theme-color meta tags in index.html, and service worker registration check.
